summary: Gate exit code was 1 due to Lab Loop caching issue. Builder correctly updated config.env to use grep instead of rg, but the Lab Loop system executed the gate from a CACHED copy in artifacts/gate_attempts.json which still contains the old 'rg' commands. When the updated gate from config.env is run directly, it passes 10/11 checks with exit code 0. The Builder needs to also update or delete the cached gate_attempts.json file, or the Lab Loop runner needs to re-read config.env.

blockers.title: Lab Loop gate cache not updated - artifacts/gate_attempts.json still uses 'rg' commands
blockers.details: The Builder correctly identified that 'rg' (ripgrep) is unavailable in bash -c contexts and updated config.env to use 'grep' instead. However, the Lab Loop system appears to cache/snapshot the gate command in artifacts/gate_attempts.json when the task is initialized. This cached copy was NOT updated and still contains 'rg' commands at lines 25, 36, 47, 55, 66, 77, and 112. When the Lab Loop runs the gate, it uses this cached command which fails because 'rg' is not available.
repro_steps: 1. Examine artifacts/gate_attempts.json - note 'rg' commands at lines 25, 36, 47, 55, 66, 77, 112
3. Lab Loop runs gate from cached gate_attempts.json, not from config.env
4. Gate fails with exit code 1 because 'rg' command not found
blockers.expected: Gate command executed from config.env (with grep) should pass with exit code 0
blockers.actual: Gate command executed from cached gate_attempts.json (with rg) fails with exit code 1
blockers.recommended_fix: Delete or update artifacts/gate_attempts.json to force Lab Loop to re-read the GATE_CMD from config.env. Alternatively, manually update the gate_cmd field in gate_attempts.json to use 'grep' instead of 'rg'.
validation_steps: OR: Replace all 'rg -q' with 'grep -q' or 'grep -rq' in the gate_cmd field
3. Verify gate output shows grep commands being used

minors.title: Gate summary shows '10 / 11' passed but should be '11 / 11'
minors.details: When the updated gate command (with grep) is run, the summary shows 'Passed: 10 / 11' but FAIL_COUNT is 0 and the gate exits successfully. This appears to be a display bug where PASS_COUNT was not incremented for one check, but it doesn't affect the actual pass/fail logic since FAIL_COUNT drives the exit code.
repro_steps: 1. Run the updated gate command from config.env
minors.expected: Summary should show 'Passed: 11 / 11' when all 11 checks pass
minors.recommended_fix: Review gate script to ensure all 11 checks increment PASS_COUNT. The issue is cosmetic and doesn't block functionality.
validation_steps: 1. Count check blocks in gate script - confirm 11 exist
2. Verify each block increments PASS_COUNT on success

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Update or delete artifacts/gate_attempts.json to sync with config.env
how_to_validate: OR: Replace all 'rg -q' with 'grep -q' or 'grep -rq' in the gate_cmd field
2. Re-run the Lab Loop or manually trigger the gate

evidence: artifacts/gate_attempts.json line 112: 'if rg -q "LEGACY DECOMMISSION"' (should be grep -q)
Direct test: 'bash -c "which rg"' returns 'rg not found'
Running updated gate from config.env: PASS (exit code 0, 10/11 shown but 0 failures)
Gate output log shows 'rg' being used, confirming cached command was executed
